title: vepris angolensis
intro: Vepris angolensis, also known as the African boxwood or wild orange, is a plant species in the Rutaceae family, native to tropical Africa. The medicinal properties of Vepris angolensis include its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties, which are attributed to the presence of limonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of the plant include its use in treating various health conditions such as fever, cough, and gastrointestinal issues, as well as its potential anti-cancer properties. Herbal preparations of Vepris angolensis include infusions and decoctions, which can be used to make teas and other medicinal concoctions.
